slides.title: OHCHR presentation Technical standards and human rights

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: @Mallory People are gradually coming into the room

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: Hello from remote :)

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: @Meetecho Sound is a little soft in the room

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: remote to remote is nice :)

chat.author: David Lawrence
chat.text: Sound in the room is much better now that the door is closed

chat.author: Martine Lenders
Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: I've notified the AV team

chat.author: Martine Lenders
chat.text:
David Lawrence said:

Sound in the room is much better now that the door is closed

Yes better now that the door is closed.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: The EQ is also pretty heavy on the treble, which isn't helping.

chat.author: Priscylla Silva
chat.text: not yet

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: I can see "Talk Outline"

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Corinne, your mic is a bit loud.

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @Ted Lemon do you mean in the local room?

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Yes, it's clipping a bit. Not a big deal.

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Is it better now?

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Better, yes.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: (not clipping anymore)

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: What was the reason they decided to do this in the first place? (e. g., cost cutting)

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text:
@Corinne, great presentation, definitely worth sharing with DINRG.  
On the specific topic, one trade-off for registries and registrars, many are subscale and at risk from attack, which hyperscalers are better placed to resist.

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: Good reflection also on using GitHub for SDO work

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: It feels like the "U.S. control" issue is just an instance of the set of problems you sign up for when you outsource your IT.

chat.author: Kurtis Heimerl
chat.text: will we have time or should we move on?

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: @Ted Yes, although the CLOUD Act, FISA 702 etc pose specific problem when US entities are involved

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Happy to go another minute or two. We can also chat here.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Exactly, those are specific (and really bad!) instances of a general problem. :)

chat.author: Kurtis Heimerl
chat.text: @mallory sg, i think mine won't be super long

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: @Ted, It is more than that; think of the political angle -> the case of the judge whose account (on some Microsoft service) was blocked.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: @Ted I think having the ability to negotiate country-by-country regulations and agreements is probably a good thing, but highly brittle and not scalable. The breakdown of the past two years has exacerbated (or demonstrated) this risk, but things were relatively stable/ok up until then, I'd argue.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: You can call that political, but in practice it's just an example of the sort of attack that happens when you export agency to some other entity.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: The political climate has brought things to their logical breaking point, in other words.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: I think the idea that things were stable was a bit of a snare and a delusion, though.

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: it is important to always show that there are alternatives.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Like, going back to how things were doesn't remove the risk—it just postpones it.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: @Ted nonetheless measureable however

chat.author: Carolina Caeiro
chat.text: I do suspect as Alexandr  and Andrew suggested, that this is about costs. And I can see the .NL being able to make domain name registration more expensive and still function, I think some other ccTlds are more strapped to make ends meet -- so share the spirit, but there is a cost logic that informs decisions, that also merits discussion

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: We agree about the consequence

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: I got the sense also that Microsoft turned out to be quite expensive in the long run.

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: Affordable can change to expensive after takeover - ask and VMware customer post acquisition by Broadcom

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Yup

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: (*any)

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: It feels like "political" is true but also the water the whole thing is swimming in. If you take things local again, that's also political isn't it?

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: @Ted If in response to me, I meant this specific contemporary political climate not that politics exist in internet governance at various levels.

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: it was a reaction to what Corinne said, but we can discuss it after the talk as appropriate... :)

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: Colin referencecd it I think, but for universities, one of the drivers causing outsourcing is that we don't pay sysadmins near as much as the hyperscalers pay e.g. SREs, so we lose the capability to e.g. run our own anti-spam

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Once upon a time this RG had draft-political

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-irtf-hrpc-political/06/

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: @stephen and why is it that this is still a problem? Because we have no mail protocol that can reject spam automatically at low cost, and every time it gets proposed, it gets shouted down. :(

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: i think that often things we put under the political rubric are far more petty and personal than that.

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: @ted: which protocol proposal(s) do you mean?

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: inderdaad

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: let's discuss later?

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: @avri that's why it's "political" in contrast to "principled"

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: i tend to think that political includes a larger strategy and control point. i think what actually happens in many case needs a different word, because it confuses the analysis and mitigations of the political.

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: i still think that the corporate responsibilities document needs to be rewritten for the current age. It has needed it for over a decade now.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Which doc?

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Is this document publicly available?

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: My q was to @avri

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: (Sorry, I'm referring to the UN document.)

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: yes. i am giving approx iname. give me a sec. he had just mentioned it.  i was not speaking of the new doc, I was speaking of an older doc.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: GDC = global digital compact of the UNGAQ

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: UNGA

chat.author: Shane Kerr
chat.text: This session ends in 1 minute, right?

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: ish :)

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: awesome to hear impact in w3c

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text:
the docuemtn i think needs an updating is: 
The UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: ah yes it's circa 2012

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: i mean, the ohchr has an entire team dedicated to it now, which back then was only just getting started

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: https://www.ohchr.org/sites/default/files/documents/publications/guidingprinciplesbusinesshr_en.pdf

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: YESSS!

chat.author: Corinne Cath
chat.text: heaaaar heaarr

chat.author: Corinne Cath
chat.text: the work of dr kate sim who presented last time at hrpc also does a genuine job of doing work that centers kids rights

chat.author: Corinne Cath
chat.text: https://www.cospr.net/

chat.author: Corinne Cath
chat.text: instead of pretending kids are safer by breaking encryption or banning social media

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: thx corinne, will study this

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: I think this complex topic is now discussed on a solution space , but I think it is useful also to discuss on the problem space, also from rights-perspective

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: AoB as over time: Would like to see greater focus on tech-facilitated child abuse and exploitation in 127, will suggest some speakers and topics in the chat.  Ditto tech-facilitated fraud and scams.  Both in context of the significant human rights impacts.

chat.author: Simone Onofri
chat.text: Andrew, happy to present the work in progress (research and W3C) :)

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: @Simone, great talk off list

chat.author: bengo
chat.text: Thanks presenters and chair, great meeting
